title: Weekly poll: will you buy the Motorola Edge 70 Max?
description: With the controversy over its software update policy now all but cleared, the Motorola Edge 70 Max stands out as a rather intriguing device – it has the most powerful chipset of the Edge 70 series, a Snapdragon 8 Gen 5, compared to Snapdragon 7 and Dimensity 8 series chips in the other phones. Its 7,100mAh battery dwarfs even the Pro/Pro+, which has a 6,500mAh battery, making this the biggest battery in the series. The 90W wired charging sure is nice too – even better is the 25W wireless charging with proper Qi 2.2 support, magnets and all. That is something that very few Android makers...

title: Weekly poll results: things are looking grim for the Nothing Phone (4b)
description: Things are looking grim for the Nothing Phone (4b) – to have a fighting chance, the phone needs a price cut and it needs it now. The results from last week’s poll show this quite clearly. Even if the phone gets good reviews – and that’s a big “if” given its specs – few people would be converted into fans. What went wrong? Well, in last week’s poll we compared the (4b) to the Nothing Phone (4a) – but one commenter says that we should have compared it to the older Nothing Phone (3a). Oof. There are many pain points – slow, non-expandable storage, relatively weak chipset, only 3 OS...

title: Samsung acknowledges the Galaxy S26 Ultra red screen problem, a fix is underway
description: Soon after the Galaxy S26 Ultra release, reports online about a red screen issue started surfacing. Up until now, no one knew what the underlying cause was, and many speculated that it was a hardware problem related to the first-generation Privacy Display OLED. However, Samsung came out with an official statement, and an executive even explained what's causing the issue. Turns out, it's not a hardware problem, and it's fixable via a software update. According to Samsung's exec, the color balance may turn reddish when the OLED is exposed to strong ambient lighting. So to tackle the...

title: Boy band BTS' J-Hope teases the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 ahead of launch
description: Samsung is hosting a Galaxy Unpacked event on July 22 in London, where it's expected to unveil its new folding smartphones, including the Galaxy Z Fold8, the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, and the Galaxy Z Flip8. We already know what these foldables will look like due to previous leaks, but we are now getting our best look yet at a working Fold8 as it's teased by BTS member J-Hope. South Korean boy band BTS' Jung Ho-seok, known professionally as J-Hope, posted a short Instagram Story on his account teasing the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8. There's nothing new to glean from this Story, but it corroborates...

title: Android lockscreen bypass allows Gemini to send SMS without verification
description: These things happen from time to time, especially when dealing with such complex software with so many edge cases and even more so with software with special privileges like Gemini’s ability to run from the lock screen. In traditional “hacking” circles, this is known as an authentication bypass vulnerability or a lockscreen bypass. It is always so fascinating to observe what things people find or often stumble upon. You can see the short video for yourself. Still, it goes something like this: The user has mindfully disabled access from Gemini to certain apps like Messages, so naturally,...

title: Apple Music and Apple One subscriptions get more expensive
description: Apple has increased the prices of Apple Music and Apple One subscriptions in the US and other countries. In the US, the individual Apple Music plan is up from $10.99 to $11.99, while the student and family plans now cost $6.99 (up from $5.99) and $19.99 (up from $16.99), respectively. The price of the individual Apple One plan remains unchanged at $19.95. But the family and premier plans got costlier by $2, as they are now priced at $27.95 and $39.95, respectively. Commenting on the price hike, Apple said, “As a result of rising licensing costs, Apple Music is increasing its...

title: Deals: Pixel 10, 10 Pro and 10 Pro XL are on sale, Nothing Phone (4a) Pro gets discounted too
description: Google is already teasing the Pixel 11 series, which will be unveiled on August 12. These could be the first phones to feature a chipset from TSMC’s 2nm fab and are expected to swap the Samsung modem for a MediaTek one. We’ve even seen leaked prices for the US market. Rumors suggest that the new Pro and Pro XL will change their base configuration to 12/256GB, losing 4GB of RAM. 16GB of RAM will be available only on the 512GB variants. That was one of the best features on the Google Pixel 10 Pro – 16GB of RAM is rare on a US flagship. You won’t get it from Samsung (unless you find a...

title: Xiaomi Mix Fold 5 spotted in the wild running HyperOS 4
description: The Xiaomi Mix Fold 5 is coming; we know that much for sure. In fact, it could arrive sooner rather than later, with many rumors speculating an August 2026 release timeframe. The device is already certified in China seemingly before any of the Xiaomi 18 family of devices, which is a good sign for a potential early release. Also, unlike the Xiaomi 18 family, the Mix Fold 5 does not need to wait for Qualcomm’s Snapdragon Elite Gen 6 chipset, since pretty much every source out there agrees that it will run on the company’s in-house Xring O3 SoC, the much improved successor to the O1 from last...

title: Google Pixel 11a specs leak
description: A fairly reputable leakster has dropped some alleged specs for Google's new "a-series" device, and if true, it might just be the best one to come out in years. There is at least one big surprise in there that we frankly didn't expect – the chipset. When Google dropped the Pixel 10a in March 2026, one of the biggest complaints most prospective buyers had was the chipset. Google decided to stick with last year's Tensor G4 chipset, which is just one of the aspects that made the 10a a bit too similar to the Pixel 9a. Well, if these new rumors are true, the Pixel 11a might actually come packing...

title: New leak by Evan Blass shows the Z Fold8, Z Fold8 Ultra and Galaxy Watch9, confirms some specs
description: At this point, we've seen and we've heard it all. The Galaxy Z Fold8, Z Fold8 Ultra and Galaxy Watch9 leaks we've seen so far leave nothing to the imagination. We've already seen some renders and most of the specs. Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra However, the latest leak comes from a reputable source, Evan Blass, and by the looks of it, all the renders are official. This, in turn, means that all the features and specs you see in the images are basically confirmed. Galaxy Z Fold8 There are no surprises when it comes to the camera hardware on the Galaxy Z Fold8...

title: Google starts sending invites for the Pixel 11 series launch event in India
description: Google sent invites to the media in the US last week for the Pixel 11 series launch event, which will be held in New York City on August 12 at 6 PM ET. Now, the Mountain View-based tech giant has sent out invites in India as well. The Google Pixel 11 series launch event in India will be hosted on August 13 in Gurugram. The invite doesn't mention when the event will begin, but it could start at 12 PM local time, after which we'll receive details on pricing and availability for the Pixel 11 series in India. Google is yet to make any announcements regarding the Pixel 11 series launch...

title: Deals: vivo X300 models on sale (with a free charger), Galaxy A37 gets a deep price cut
description: Amazon is running a promo that gives you a free 90W FlashCharge adapter with one of several high-end models. This is on top of regular discounts, of course. The vivo X300 Pro, for example, is 16% off. This model is equipped with a 50MP 1/1.28” main camera, a 200MP 3.7x/85mm periscope and a 50MP ultra-wide. It is powered by the Dimensity 9500 and a 5,440mAh battery with 90W wired and 40W wireless charging. The screen is a 6.78” 120Hz LTPO panel with Dolby Vision. vivo X300 Pro €230 offfree 90W charger ...

title: Huawei Pura 90s Pro Max in for review
description: We brought you a hands-on with the Huawei Pura 90s Pro Max from the launch event in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia. Now, it's time to bring the phone to our headquarters and start its review. The phone ships with a USB cable, a clear hard case, and a 100W charger in the box. There may be variations of this, depending on the location, similar to the situation with the battery - European customers will get a 5,500mAh battery, while elsewhere, units will have a 6,000mAh power pack. Unboxing the Pura 90s Pro Max We already went into detail with the phone in our hands-on. It's very...

title: Motorola issues an official statement on the Edge 70 Max's software update policy
description: Following up on our editorial regarding its incredibly confusing software support policy for the recently announced Edge 70 Max, Motorola reached out to us to clarify the matter. The company rep, who contacted us following our story, had this to say: We can confirm that Edge 70 Max arrives with up to 3 OS upgrades and up to 5 years of SMR. Lenovo and Motorola Mobility are committed to meeting the principles set out in the Lot X regulation and will continue to provide security, corrective and functionality updates to our phones and tablets, to help ensure the longevity and operation of...

title: New proof for the iPhone 18 Pro having a variable aperture camera surfaces
description: Indian Apple partner Tata Electronics was hacked by a ransomware group last month, and that resulted in the outing of a ton of internal company files, which some people have been sifting through ever since. Now a diagnostics log has been found in the files, and this confirms the much-rumored fact that the iPhone 18 Pro and iPhone 18 Pro Max will have a main camera with variable aperture. The log also confirms that Apple is using a new sensor for the aforementioned devices' main camera, namely Sony's IMX905. Its pixel size remains identical to its predecessor’s at 1.22μm, which seems to...

title: Global Redmi Note 17 Pro's memory options revealed
description: Redmi unveiled the Note 17 Pro alongside the Note 17 for the Chinese market a few days ago, and it's obviously also working on their global models. In fact, data from an import and export database reveals that the global Redmi Note 17 Pro will be available in at least two RAM/storage configurations: 4/64GB and 6/128GB. For reference, note that the Chinese Redmi Note 17 Pro is available with 8GB and 12GB of RAM and 128/256/512GB of storage. The global device has the model number 2607DRA18G, whereas the Chinese version is 2607DRA18C. The trailing letter denotes the market it’s intended for,...

title: vivo is deciding between three 200MP sensors for the X500 Ultra's telephoto
description: The vivo X500 Ultra isn't expected any time soon, and it will likely arrive on the global stage in early 2027, but that doesn't stop the rumor mill from spinning. The latest leak comes from the renowned Weibo tipster Digital Chat Station, and it's all about the telephoto camera. According to the report, vivo's imaging team still hasn't decided on the right 200MP sensor for the periscope telephoto camera. Engineers are split between three sensor sizes - 1/1.12", 1/1.3" and 1/1.4". Of course, the larger sensor means better low-light performance and generally better image quality, but it...

title: Lava Virat V1 and Virat V1 5G's specs revealed, launch rescheduled
description: Lava announced a partnership with Flipkart last week for its new Virat smartphone series and said the Virat V1 would launch in India on July 21. Well, the launch has been rescheduled to July 24, and Lava has announced it will unveil the Virat V1 and Virat V1 5G as the first smartphones in the Virat series next Friday. Additionally, Lava and Flipkart revealed the specs of both Virat series smartphones. The Lava Virat V1 5G will be powered by the Unisoc T8200 SoC, have 4GB RAM and 64GB of onboard storage, and run Android 16 out of the box without ads or bloatware. It will feature a 6.75"...

title: The new 12.2-inch Asus Pad is on its way to India, here's when it lands
description: Last month, Asus re-entered the Android tablet market by unveiling the 12.2-inch Asus Pad. Today, the company has confirmed that the device is headed to India, where it will launch on August 6 at 12:00 noon local time. Unfortunately, the price hasn't been revealed yet. The Asus Pad's screen uses OLED technology and has a resolution of 2800x1840, a 144Hz refresh rate, a 3:2 aspect ratio, and 2,000-nit peak brightness. The tablet has four speakers which support Dolby Atmos 360, and it's powered by the MediaTek Dimensity 8300 SoC, paired with 8GB of LPDDR5X RAM and 128GB or 256GB of...

title: RedMagic Astra 2 review
